title: Pipeline wiring and end-to-end integration
analysis:
The orchestrator.py currently registers all 9 stages as stubs (_stage_stub) that log 'not yet implemented'. The CLI wires 7 subcommands (ingest, analyze, plan, edit, render, upload, run) that delegate to these stubs via orch._stage_map[NAME].func(). All downstream module implementations are complete with well-defined public APIs.

The task requires: (1) Replace each stub with a real stage function that calls the actual module APIs, (2) Implement progress reporting with stage timings and wall-clock budget tracking, (3) Handle human-review checkpoint in NARRATE stage, (4) Per-narrative error isolation in multi-narrative stages (SCRIPT, EDL, SOURCE, RENDER, UPLOAD), (5) Wire CLI subcommands to use the real stage functions, (6) Create an e2e test.

Key function signatures to wire:
- INGEST: scanner.scan_directory(input_dir, max_workers=None) -> list[MediaFile], normalizer.normalize_audio(media_path, output_dir, root_dir=None) -> Path, dedup.mark_duplicates(catalog_db) -> int
- ANALYZE: asr.transcribe_media(media_id, audio_path, db, scheduler, config, batch_size=24), scenes.detect_shots(media_id, video_path, db, scheduler), objects.detect_objects(media_id, video_path, db, scheduler, config, sparse=False), faces.detect_faces(media_id, video_path, db, scheduler, config), embeddings.compute_embeddings(media_id, video_path, db, scheduler, config), audio_events.classify_audio_events(media_id, audio_path, db, scheduler), faces.cluster_faces(db, eps=0.5, min_samples=3)
- CLASSIFY: cluster.cluster_activities(db) -> list[ActivityCluster], classify.label_activities(db, config: LLMConfig)
- NARRATE: narratives.build_master_storyboard(db) -> str, narratives.propose_narratives(storyboard, db, config: AutopilotConfig) -> list[Narrative], narratives.format_for_review(narratives) -> str
- SCRIPT: script.generate_script(narrative_id, db, config: LLMConfig) -> dict
- EDL: edl.generate_edl(narrative_id, db, config: LLMConfig) -> dict, validator.validate_edl(edl, db) -> ValidationResult, otio_export.export_otio(edl, output_path, db) -> Path
- SOURCE: resolve.resolve_edl_assets(edl, config: ModelConfig, output_dir, db, narrative_id=None) -> dict
- RENDER: router.route_and_render(narrative_id, db, config: OutputConfig) -> Path, validate.validate_render(rendered_path, edl, config: OutputConfig) -> ValidationReport
- UPLOAD: youtube.upload_video(narrative_id, video_path, db, config: YouTubeConfig) -> str, thumbnail.extract_best_thumbnail(narrative_id, video_path, db) -> Path

The existing test_orchestrator.py (395 lines, 12 test classes) and test_cli.py (430 lines) have comprehensive tests for the current stub-based behavior. New tests must extend these without breaking existing ones.

prerequisites.description: Add e2e test fixtures to conftest.py: a minimal_config fixture that creates an AutopilotConfig with tmp_path-based directories, and a mock_gpu_scheduler fixture that provides a no-op GPUScheduler-like context manager.

steps.description: Write failing test in test_orchestrator.py: TestIngestStage class with test_ingest_calls_scan_directory (mock scanner.scan_directory returns list of MediaFile, verify called with config.input_dir), test_ingest_inserts_media_into_db (verify db.insert_media called for each scanned file), test_ingest_normalizes_audio (mock normalizer.normalize_audio called for each media file), test_ingest_marks_duplicates (verify dedup.mark_duplicates called with db).

Implement _run_ingest(config, db) in orchestrator.py: imports scanner, normalizer, dedup. Calls scan_directory(config.input_dir), inserts each MediaFile into db via db.insert_media(), calls normalize_audio for each media file with audio (output_dir=config.output_dir/'normalized', root_dir=config.input_dir), then calls mark_duplicates(db). Replace the INGEST stub.

Write failing test in test_orchestrator.py: TestAnalyzeStage class with test_analyze_creates_gpu_scheduler (mock GPUScheduler, verify instantiated with config.processing params), test_analyze_runs_all_analysis_per_media (mock all 6 analysis functions, verify each called for each media in DB), test_analyze_calls_cluster_faces_after_analysis (verify cluster_faces called once after all per-media analysis), test_analyze_runs_audio_and_video_concurrently (verify ThreadPoolExecutor used for parallel audio/video analysis).

Implement _run_analyze(config, db) in orchestrator.py: creates GPUScheduler, lists all media from DB with status!='duplicate', for each media file runs video analysis (scenes, objects, faces, embeddings) and audio analysis (asr, audio_events) using concurrent.futures.ThreadPoolExecutor for parallelism, then calls cluster_faces(db). Replace the ANALYZE stub.

Write failing test in test_orchestrator.py: TestClassifyStage class with test_classify_calls_cluster_activities (mock cluster.cluster_activities, verify called with db), test_classify_calls_label_activities (mock classify.label_activities, verify called with db and config.llm).
Implement _run_classify(config, db) in orchestrator.py: calls cluster_activities(db), then label_activities(db, config.llm). Replace the CLASSIFY stub.

Write failing test in test_orchestrator.py: TestNarrateStage class with test_narrate_builds_storyboard (mock build_master_storyboard, verify called with db), test_narrate_proposes_narratives (mock propose_narratives, verify called with storyboard, db, config), test_narrate_calls_human_review_callback (verify human_review_fn is called with formatted text and list of Narrative objects), test_narrate_auto_approves_when_no_callback (without human_review_fn all narratives get status='approved'), test_narrate_respects_review_rejections (human_review_fn returns subset of IDs, rejected ones get status='rejected').

Implement _run_narrate(config, db, human_review_fn=None) in orchestrator.py: calls build_master_storyboard(db), propose_narratives(storyboard, db, config), format_for_review(narratives), invokes human_review_fn(formatted, narratives) if provided (returns approved narrative_ids), defaults to all-approved if no callback. Updates statuses in DB. Replace the NARRATE stub.

Write failing test in test_orchestrator.py: TestScriptStage class with test_script_generates_per_approved_narrative (mock generate_script, seed DB with 2 approved narratives, verify called once per narrative), test_script_continues_on_per_narrative_error (first narrative raises ScriptError, second still gets generated), test_script_raises_if_all_narratives_fail (if every narrative fails, stage itself raises).

Implement _run_script(config, db) in orchestrator.py: lists narratives with status='approved', calls generate_script(narrative_id, db, config.llm) for each with try/except per narrative. Logs errors but continues. Raises RuntimeError only if ALL narratives failed. Replace the SCRIPT stub.

Write failing test in test_orchestrator.py: TestEdlStage class with test_edl_generates_validates_exports_per_narrative (mock generate_edl, validate_edl, export_otio, verify all 3 called for each narrative with a script), test_edl_stores_validation_result (verify db.upsert_edit_plan stores validation_json), test_edl_continues_on_failure (one narrative fails, others still processed).

Implement _run_edl(config, db) in orchestrator.py: for each approved narrative with a script, calls generate_edl(narrative_id, db, config.llm), validate_edl(edl, db), export_otio(edl, config.output_dir/narrative_id/timeline.otio, db). Stores results via db.upsert_edit_plan. Per-narrative error isolation. Replace the EDL stub.

Write failing test in test_orchestrator.py: TestSourceStage class with test_source_resolves_assets_per_narrative (mock resolve_edl_assets, verify called for each narrative with an edit plan, passing correct args: edl dict, config.models, output_dir, db, narrative_id).

Implement _run_source_assets(config, db) in orchestrator.py: for each narrative with an edit plan, loads EDL JSON from db.get_edit_plan, calls resolve_edl_assets(edl, config.models, config.output_dir/'assets'/narrative_id, db, narrative_id=narrative_id). Per-narrative error isolation. Replace the SOURCE_ASSETS stub.

Write failing test in test_orchestrator.py: TestRenderStage class with test_render_routes_and_validates_per_narrative (mock route_and_render and validate_render, verify both called per narrative), test_render_logs_validation_warnings (verify validation issues are logged), test_render_continues_on_failure (one narrative's render fails, others still processed).

Implement _run_render(config, db) in orchestrator.py: for each narrative with an edit plan, calls route_and_render(narrative_id, db, config.output), then validate_render(output_path, edl, config.output). Logs validation issues. Per-narrative error isolation. Replace the RENDER stub.

Write failing test in test_orchestrator.py: TestUploadStage class with test_upload_uploads_and_thumbnails_per_narrative (mock upload_video and extract_best_thumbnail, verify both called per narrative), test_upload_continues_on_failure (one narrative fails, others still uploaded).

Implement _run_upload(config, db) in orchestrator.py: for each narrative with a rendered output (check for render output path in edit_plan or a convention), calls upload_video(narrative_id, video_path, db, config.youtube) then extract_best_thumbnail(narrative_id, video_path, db). Per-narrative error isolation. Replace the UPLOAD stub.

Write failing test in test_orchestrator.py: TestRealStageRegistration class with test_orchestrator_registers_real_functions_not_stubs (verify each stage's func is not a stub by checking it doesn't log 'not yet implemented'), test_orchestrator_accepts_human_review_fn_parameter (PipelineOrchestrator(human_review_fn=callback) stores it and passes to NARRATE stage).

Update PipelineOrchestrator.__init__ to register the real stage functions (_run_ingest, _run_analyze, etc.) instead of stubs. Add human_review_fn parameter that gets passed through to _run_narrate. The NARRATE stage func wraps _run_narrate with the callback via functools.partial or a lambda.

Write failing test in test_orchestrator.py: TestEnhancedProgress class with test_run_logs_cumulative_elapsed_per_stage (after each stage, log shows cumulative time and remaining budget), test_run_logs_summary_table_at_end (final log message includes all stage names with their elapsed times).

Enhance PipelineOrchestrator.run() progress reporting: after each stage completion log cumulative elapsed vs budget (e.g., '[PROGRESS] 1200.0s / 3600.0s budget (33.3%)'), and at the end emit a summary table listing each stage with its elapsed time and status.

Write failing tests in test_cli.py: TestCLIRealStageWiring class with test_cli_run_uses_real_orchestrator (invoke 'run' with mocked stage modules, verify real stage functions are called not stubs), test_cli_ingest_calls_real_stage (invoke 'ingest', verify _run_ingest path is exercised), test_cli_individual_subcommands_call_correct_stages (each subcommand exercises the right combination of stage functions).

Update autopilot/cli.py: since PipelineOrchestrator now registers real functions, the existing CLI subcommands that call orch._stage_map[NAME].func(config=config, db=db) will automatically use the real implementations. Verify all subcommands work with the updated orchestrator. No major CLI changes needed beyond ensuring the 'run' subcommand passes human_review_fn (default to interactive input() prompt for CLI use).

Write tests/test_e2e.py: comprehensive end-to-end test using in-memory DB and mocked external dependencies. TestEndToEnd class with test_full_pipeline_smoke (mock all external calls — scanner returns fake MediaFile list, all ML functions write to DB, LLM calls return canned responses, ffmpeg mocked, YouTube API mocked — run full pipeline, verify DB state: media inserted, transcripts exist, detections stored, clusters created, narratives proposed and approved, scripts generated, EDLs stored, render output path returned, upload record created).

Fix any integration issues discovered during e2e test execution. Adjust stage function data flow, argument passing, or error handling as needed to ensure the full pipeline runs end-to-end with mocks. Ensure all existing tests in test_orchestrator.py and test_cli.py still pass.

design_decisions.decision: Stage functions as module-level functions (_run_ingest, _run_analyze, etc.) in orchestrator.py
design_decisions.rationale: Each stage function is defined as a module-level function rather than a method on PipelineOrchestrator. This keeps them testable independently, follows the existing pattern where stage.func is a callable accepting (config, db) kwargs, and avoids making the PipelineOrchestrator class too large.

design_decisions.decision: Human review via callback function pattern (human_review_fn parameter)
design_decisions.rationale: The NARRATE stage accepts an optional human_review_fn callback rather than using input() directly. This allows the CLI to provide an interactive prompt, tests to provide an auto-approve mock, and future UIs to provide their own implementation. Default behavior is auto-approve-all when no callback is provided. Signature: human_review_fn(formatted_text: str, narratives: list[Narrative]) -> list[str] (approved narrative IDs).

design_decisions.decision: Per-narrative error isolation via try/except in multi-narrative stages
design_decisions.rationale: SCRIPT, EDL, SOURCE_ASSETS, RENDER, and UPLOAD stages iterate over narratives. If processing one narrative fails, we catch the exception, log it, and continue with the remaining narratives. The stage only raises if ALL narratives fail. This prevents a single bad narrative from blocking the entire pipeline, as specified in the task requirements.

design_decisions.decision: GPUScheduler created internally in ANALYZE stage, not passed through orchestrator
design_decisions.rationale: Rather than threading a scheduler through the orchestrator interface, the ANALYZE stage creates a GPUScheduler internally using config.processing settings (gpu_device, total_vram from nvidia-smi query or sensible default). This encapsulates GPU resource management within the single stage that needs it, keeping the universal stage interface clean at (config, db).

design_decisions.decision: ANALYZE runs audio and video analysis concurrently via ThreadPoolExecutor
design_decisions.rationale: Audio stages (ASR, audio_events) primarily use CPU for preprocessing and a different GPU model than video stages. Using concurrent.futures.ThreadPoolExecutor for per-media analysis allows overlapping audio and video processing. The GPUScheduler's LRU eviction and reference counting handle model loading conflicts.

design_decisions.decision: E2e test mocks at module boundaries, not at function internals
design_decisions.rationale: The e2e test mocks external dependencies at the top-level function boundary (e.g., patch scanner.scan_directory to return fake MediaFiles, patch transcribe_media to write directly to DB). This tests the full orchestrator wiring and data flow through the DB while avoiding slow/flaky ML model loading and external API calls.

design_decisions.decision: Preserve backward compatibility of PipelineOrchestrator constructor
design_decisions.rationale: The existing constructor PipelineOrchestrator(budget_seconds=None) is used in both CLI and tests. Adding human_review_fn as an optional keyword argument maintains backward compatibility. All existing tests that create PipelineOrchestrator() without arguments will continue to work, using auto-approve behavior.

design_decisions.decision: Individual CLI subcommands continue using orch._stage_map pattern
design_decisions.rationale: The CLI subcommands (ingest, analyze, etc.) already access stage functions via orch._stage_map[NAME].func(). Since the orchestrator now registers real functions instead of stubs, these calls automatically use real implementations. Minimal CLI changes are needed — just ensure the orchestrator constructor receives appropriate parameters.

reuse.how: Reuse the existing catalog_db fixture for all new stage tests and the e2e test. It provides an in-memory SQLite DB with autocommit for test convenience.
Reuse the _write_minimal_config helper and the CliRunner-based test pattern with patch('autopilot.cli.*') for CLI wiring tests.

reuse.what: MagicMock stage replacement pattern from test_orchestrator.py
reuse.how: Reuse the pattern of replacing stage.func with MagicMock for testing orchestrator behavior. Extend with targeted patching for specific stage function verification.

Create a GPUScheduler instance in the ANALYZE stage using config.processing.gpu_device. Pass it to all analysis functions that accept a scheduler parameter (asr, scenes, objects, faces, embeddings, audio_events).
The SOURCE_ASSETS stage delegates entirely to resolve_edl_assets(), which already orchestrates music, broll, and voiceover sourcing plus fetch list generation. No need to duplicate that logic.
The EDL stage calls validate_edl(edl, db) which returns a ValidationResult with pass/fail and issues. Store the validation result in the edit_plans table alongside the EDL.
The RENDER stage calls validate_render(output_path, edl, config.output) after route_and_render() to verify quality. Log issues as warnings but don't fail the stage on warnings.
Continue using the existing types for run() return value and stage registration. Enhanced progress reporting adds cumulative timing but doesn't change these types.
The NARRATE stage calls build_master_storyboard(db) to create the storyboard text, and format_for_review(narratives) to create human-readable review text for the callback.
